cohorts.label: 15 potentially interested parties contacted by Barclays, including 11 strategic companies and four financial sponsors
Six parties that executed confidentiality agreements and attended meetings with Company management
Three parties that submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest on June 9, 2016
Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, the two parties advanced to final round with bid deadline of July 8, 2016

observations.summary: Imprivata completed its initial public offering at $15.00 per share
In early 2015 and again in June 2015, Thoma Bravo informally approached the Company and expressed interest in a possible business transaction; no specific proposals were made
In January 2016, Thoma Bravo met with Company management at an industry conference and reiterated interest in a potential transaction; no specific proposal was made

Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter to the Board at $15.00 per share in cash, with equity financing and ability to complete confirmatory diligence in 30 days or less

Board held a meeting to consider Thoma Bravo's indication of interest; deferred consideration pending further review of Company strategies and intrinsic value; authorized advisory committee to recommend a financial advisor
observations.other_detail: Board deferred consideration of Thoma Bravo IOI; authorized advisory committee to seek financial advisor

observations.summary: Board engaged Barclays as financial advisor to assist in evaluation of Thoma Bravo's indication of interest and any other strategic alternatives
Company countersigned engagement letter with Barclays, dated April 15, 2016
Board concluded Thoma Bravo's indication of interest undervalued the Company; requested Barclays assemble list of other potentially interested parties
observations.other_detail: Board concluded IOI undervalued the Company; directed Barclays to assemble buyer list

observations.summary: Board reviewed Barclays' indicative list of 15 potentially interested parties (11 strategic companies and four financial sponsors including Thoma Bravo); discussed risks and benefits of commencing a process
observations.other_detail: Board reviewed indicative buyer list of 15 parties

observations.summary: Board approved the indicative list of 15 parties and determined Barclays should contact each on a confidential basis to gauge interest; formal launch of the strategic process
Barclays began contacting the 15 potentially interested parties to gauge interest in a potential transaction; period from May 6 through June 9, 2016
Thoma Bravo executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company; three strategic parties and four financial sponsors total executed NDAs during May 6 through June 9 period
Board approved requesting initial non-binding indications of interest from bidders by June 9, 2016

Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter to the six parties that had executed a confidentiality agreement and attended meetings with management; deadline for indications of interest was June 9, 2016
One strategic party that did not execute a confidentiality agreement informed Barclays it was not interested in participating in the strategic process
Strategic 1 informed Barclays it was no longer interested in participating in the strategic process and would not be submitting an indication of interest
Sponsor A presented a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $16.50 per share, with equity commitment for the entire purchase price and not contingent on financing

Thoma Bravo presented a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $17.25 per share, with equity commitment for the entire purchase price, not contingent on financing, and also provided a form of equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement

Sponsor B presented a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at a range of $17.00 - $18.00 per share, with equity commitment for the entire purchase price and not contingent on financing
One strategic party that did not execute a confidentiality agreement informed Barclays it did not have a strategic interest in an acquisition of the Company
Strategic 2 informed Barclays that because of other internal corporate priorities, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company
Board authorized Barclays to advance Sponsor A, Sponsor B, and Thoma Bravo to second phase and to encourage them to increase offer prices

Strategic 3 informed Barclays that because of its internal focus on other corporate transactions and a perceived overlap in technologies, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company

Sponsor A informed Barclays it would not meaningfully improve upon its June 9, 2016 preliminary indication of interest; Barclays informed Sponsor A the Board would not be interested at that level; no further discussions occurred

Board established the Special Committee of independent and disinterested directors to consider and evaluate indications of interest and direct negotiations; members: David Barrett, Paul Maeder, Kate Walsh, Jack Blaeser, Dr. John Halamka and Rodger Weismann
Special Committee confirmed it was not interested in pursuing a transaction at the price indicated by Sponsor A in its initial bid; Sponsor A was going to cease participating in the strategic process
Special Committee directed Barclays to contact Strategic 4 to determine whether it might have interest in submitting an indication of interest
Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo were provided access to an online data room containing nonpublic information regarding the Company
observations.other_detail: Data room access granted to second-phase bidders

observations.summary: Strategic 4 informed Barclays that an acquisition of the Company would not be a strategic fit and it was not interested in participating in the strategic process
Chairperson of the Special Committee approved Barclays to set a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016

Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company's proposed form of merger agreement by July 7, 2016, and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016

Sponsor B informed Barclays that if it were to submit a final bid it would be significantly below its June 9, 2016 preliminary indication of interest; Barclays indicated the Special Committee would not be interested; no further discussions occurred
Company's proposed form of merger agreement and voting agreement was made available to Thoma Bravo
observations.other_detail: Draft merger agreement provided to Thoma Bravo

observations.summary: Thoma Bravo's outside counsel Kirkland sent a revised draft of the merger agreement to Goodwin

Thoma Bravo submitted its final bid at $19.00 per share in cash, with equity commitment for the entire purchase price; indicated it had completed all due diligence and was prepared to execute the merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to market open on July 11, 2016; Sponsor B did not submit a bid
Barclays contacted Thoma Bravo and informed it to submit its best and final offer, which the Special Committee expected would be an improvement over the $19.00 bid

Thoma Bravo submitted a revised written, non-binding best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price; Thoma Bravo made it clear that $19.25 per share was its best and final offer
Representatives of Goodwin and Kirkland negotiated open items in the merger agreement, voting agreement and equity commitment letter; negotiations continued through execution on July 13, 2016
observations.other_detail: Merger agreement negotiation between Company and Thoma Bravo counsel

observations.summary: Kirkland sent Goodwin a draft exclusivity agreement with term extending through July 13, 2016; the Company declined to enter into an exclusivity agreement
observations.other_detail: proposed by Thoma Bravo; rejected by Company

observations.summary: Barclays delivered its oral fairness opinion to the Special Committee that the $19.25 per share cash consideration was fair from a financial point of view
Special Committee unanimously recommended to the Board that the Company enter into the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo at $19.25 per share
observations.other_detail: Special Committee unanimous recommendation to Board

observations.summary: Board unanimously adopted and declared the advisability of the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo and recommended stockholder approval
observations.other_detail: Board unanimous approval and stockholder recommendation

observations.summary: Parties finalized and executed the merger agreement and received executed final copies of the equity commitment letter and the voting agreements; Company and Thoma Bravo issued a joint press release announcing the execution
Strategic 3 executed a confidentiality agreement and met with management but did not submit a bid by the June 9, 2016 deadline

exclusions.explanation: Thoma Bravo open market share purchases (August-October 2015): Stock accumulation activity, not an observation type in the v2 schema (not a proposal, solicitation, agreement, process event, status, or outcome)
Company Q3 2015 earnings announcement and stock price decline (October-November 2015): Company financial performance and stock price context; not an M&A observation type
Company secondary offering (August 11, 2015): Capital markets event, not part of the M&A process chronology
Senior VP of worldwide sales departure announcement (June 1, 2016): Personnel change, not an M&A observation type; referenced in deliberations but not itself a process event
Detailed management presentations to bidders (June 27-28, 2016): Due diligence process events already captured in the broader solicitation/process context; not separately modeled as observation types

Thoma Bravo request for CEO discussion prior to second round bid (July 6, 2016) and its denial: Process negotiation detail; the Special Committee denied the request but this is an internal process detail rather than a distinct observation type

Kirkland request for Company to contact large stockholder for voting agreement (July 11, 2016): Negotiation detail regarding voting agreement logistics; Company declined to contact stockholder pre-execution
February 24, 2016 Board meeting discussing Company performance: Board discussion of operating/financial performance without M&A action; no proposal, agreement, or process decision was made
Thoma Bravo request to work with potential debt financing sources (July 5-6, 2016): Financing logistics detail; two debt financing sources joined under the existing NDA
Strategic 3 still evaluating interest on June 11, 2016: Interim status that did not result in a bid; Strategic 3 formally declined on June 14 which is captured
